subject: refactor(debug): extract console patch into src/debug/consoleCapture.ts
body:
debugLog.ts was carrying three distinct concerns: the pub/sub event
store, environment + report generation, and the console.error /
console.warn runtime patch. The console patch block (describeConsoleArgs
+ PATCHED_MARK sentinel + wrappedError / wrappedWarn + reentrant flag)
is ~60 lines of single-purpose code with its own idempotency semantics
— clean extraction candidate.

- New: src/debug/consoleCapture.ts exports installConsoleCapture(),
  which checks Platform.OS === 'web' at runtime and no-ops otherwise.
  Single file (no .web.ts + .ts split) because (a) the platform gate is
  a one-line runtime check, not a bundle-split concern, and (b) keeping
  it single-file means the existing jest tests exercise the real
  implementation via the react-native mock without needing
  jest-specific .web resolution config.
- debugLog.ts imports installConsoleCapture and calls it once after
  the boot debugAdd. Circular import is safe — consoleCapture's
  required deps (debugAdd, safeStringify) are hoisted function
  exports, available when consoleCapture evaluates.
- Removed describeConsoleArgs + PATCHED_MARK + the patch block from
  debugLog.ts.

Before: debugLog.ts 386 lines, console patch inline.
After: debugLog.ts 345 lines, consoleCapture.ts 88 lines.

No behaviour change. The jest tests continue to cover the capture
path end-to-end (console.error call → debug entry), so the refactor
is transparent to the tests. 83/83 passing. tsc 0. expo web build
clean.

Tags: debug, refactor, modularity
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: debug-pill-hardening
